description: A small pistol accepting Glock magazines and chambered in 10mm.  Because of its size, it is easy to carry, but it has rather fierce recoil.

variants.description: A modestly sized version of the highly successful Glock, chambered in hard-hitting 10mm Auto.  It is easy to carry because of its small size, but also recoils fiercely.

description: A semi-automatic pistol chambered in 10mm and accepting Glock magazines.

variants.description: A full-sized version of the highly successful Glock, chambered in hard-hitting 10mm Auto.  The third model of Glock designed, all other full-sized, large framed Glocks are adaptations of this one.

description: A full-sized, long-slide version of the highly successful Glock, chambered in hard-hitting 10mm Auto.  The extra barrel length makes for slightly more accurate and powerful shots, and the extra slide length means the sights are a little easier to use.  It also features Glock's Modular Optic System, making aftermarket optics much easier to mount.

variants.description: A full-sized, long-slide version of the highly successful Glock, chambered in hard-hitting 10mm Auto.  The extra barrel length makes for slightly more accurate and powerful shots, and the extra slide length means the sights are a little easier to use.

//: Yes, I know it's not really a glock smg.
It takes glock magazines, and it's own special one that I am also renaming to a glock magazine
so, I'm calling it a glock smg.
description: A submachine gun chambered in 10mm, accepting Glock magazines.

variants.description: The Vector SDP is a delayed blowback "pistol" with a unique, downward reciprocating bolt that helps combat muzzle climb.  This one is chambered in 10mm Auto.

description: A Glock chambered in .22LR.  While effective against paper targets and rodents, it will struggle against the walking dead.

The smallest gun Glock produces, chambered in .380 ACP.  Even though it is small a small gun that shoots a weak cartridge, it is still effective against small game, paper targets, and zombified children
A .40 S&W variant of the popular Glock 17 pistol.  The standard-issue firearm of the FBI from 1997-2016, and of countless other law enforcement agencies worldwide.
A compact version of the Glock 22, chambered in .40 S&W.  Popular among civilian and law enforcement alike for its capacity, durability, and reliability.

A diminutive pistol chambered in .40 S&W. Widely popular among concealed carriers, its popularity soared further in the months leading up to the Cataclysm, partly due to its ability to fit the higher cpacity magazines of its older brothers.

//: Yes, I know it's not really a glock smg.
It takes glock magazines, and it's own special one that I am also renaming to a glock magazine
so, I'm calling it a glock smg.
description: A submachine gun chambered in .45 ACP, accepting Glock magazines.

variants.description: The Vector SMG is a delayed blowback submachine gun with a unique, in-line design that places both the shooter's hand and shoulder in line with the bore axis, reducing muzzle climb during bursts.  This one is chambered in .45 ACP.

description: A semi-automatic pistol chambered in .45 ACP and accepting Glock magazines.

variants.description: A full-sized version of the highly successful Glock.  This model is chambered in .45 ACP.

description: A Glock compact pistol chambered in .45 ACP.  Accepts the same magazines as its older brothers.
A Glock subcompact pistol chambered in .45 ACP.  Due to its slim construction, it only accepts Glock 36 magazines.

A common 9x19mm pistol of modest proportions, produced by the popular Glock gun company and mainly built from plastic polymers.  Making use of aptly-named Glock magazines and being a decades-long popular concealed carry option for civilians due to its small size, it is a tried, tested, and reliable design.

variants.description: Possibly the most popular pistol in existence, the Glock 19 can be summed up as being fundamentally a more compact brother to the full-sized Glock 17 pistol.  Sporting a mainly polymer-based design, the Glock 19 is often derided for its plastic construction which lends the handgun a deceptively flimsy feel.  Even so, the Glock 19 remains both a remarkably reliable firearm and a quite shootable gun in its own right.

A Glock 19 developed specially for the FBI, with all black internals and match grade components.  It was nearly impossible to find on the civilian market before the Cataclysm, now it is completely irreplacable.
The Glock 45 is practically identical to the Glock 19, the main difference being that the grip is styled to be closer to that of the Glock 17.

description: A Glock subcompact pistol chambered in 9mm Luger.  Despite its tiny size, it can fit the magazines of its larger brothers.
A large, stylish Glock chambered in 9mm.  Developed for accuracy, this pistol is used by competition shooters, law enforcement, and even the Baba Yaga.
A 9x19mm Glock of diminutive proportions.  Unfortunately it is incompatible with other 9mm Glock magazines.

//: This one uses 10 round doublestack mags, which are incompatible with the standard variant
description: A 9mm compact Glock.  Despite its name, it does not accept the magazines of the Glock 43.
A 9mm, slimline Glock.  Designed for concealed carry, and accepts the magazine from a Glock 43x, but not the base 43.

With its claim to fame being its ease of portability when compared to other conventional rifles and carbines, this is a uniquely-designed pistol caliber carbine sporting an integral folding mechanism and chambered for 9x19mm cartridges, making use of regular Glock compatible magazines.  With its mechanism engaged, the barrel assembly swings rearwards and causes the firearm to fold to half its regular length, packing up into a very compact package with ergonomics unfortunately having to take a backseat to allow for the weapon's extreme portability.  With a mainly polymer constructed receiver, the carbine is relatively light and incredibly transportable when fully folded.

variants.description: With its claim to fame being its ease of portability when compared to other conventional rifles and carbines, the Kel-Tec SUB-2000 is a uniquely-designed pistol caliber carbine sporting an integral folding mechanism and chambered for 9x19mm cartridges, making use of regular Glock compatible magazines.  With its mechanism engaged, the barrel assembly swings rearwards and causes the firearm to fold to half its regular length, packing up into a very compact package with ergonomics unfortunately having to take a backseat to allow for the weapon's extreme portability.  With a mainly polymer constructed receiver, the carbine is relatively light and incredibly transportable when fully folded.

//: The actual gun is around 30 cm longer than what's depicted here, but the inbuilt accessory adds 35 cm, which ends up totalling to the same length. IRL the SUB-2000 folds literally in half, which is a greatly smaller size than what our folding stocks can collapse to, so like this the length stays the same as far as the player can see, and you still get the same largely folded effect as you'd have on the real gun.
//1: Assuming gen 3 to allow sights to be mounted and folded

description: Designed for all shooters, this offering from the Glock gun company is a full-sized 9x19mm pistol marketed towards law-enforcement and military users.  Because of the long magazine well, it can't fit magazines shorter than the standard 17-round ones.

variants.description: Commonly seen tucked away in the holsters of police officers, certain military professionals, and civilian shooters, this is a larger, older, service-oriented version of the popular Glock 19.  Similarly firing the 9x19mm cartridge and using higher capacity magazines, it performs largely the same, but due to the longer magazine well, it can't fit magazines shorter than the standard 17-round ones.

//2: Glock 17s cannot load magazines shorter than the standard 17rd magazine.

description: A military, fully automatic capable version of the popular 9x19mm handguns produced by the Glock arms company, this machine pistol makes use of Glock magazines.  The weapon has had its barrel modified to lessen recoil, a useful feature considering its high rate of fire and lack of a default buttstock.

variants.description: A selective-fire variation on the Glock 17, originally designed for Austria's EKO Cobra unit.  It has compensator cuts along its barrel to make recoil more manageable.

description: A lightweight pistol caliber carbine, chambered in 9mm and accepting Glock magazines.

variants.description: The Ruger PCC is a pistol caliber carbine, made by Ruger.  It's quite commonplace, thanks to its low recoil, reliability, and use of readily available magazines.

//: Assuming glock magwell, the other magwell for Ruger magazines can be added with the ruger pistol

description: A .22 Long Rifle rifle that chambers just one shot.  Its main use is for the youth, and not much else.  God help you if you hope to use this thing to a meaningful effect.

variants.description: A .22 Long Rifle rifle that chambers just one shot.  Its main use is for the youth, and not much else.  God help you if you hope to use this thing to a meaningful effect.

description: A very accurate rifle chambered for the powerful .308 round.

variants.description: An ubiquitous bolt action rifle, the Model 700 is used by police agencies, militaries, and civilians alike around the globe.  Simple, accurate, and cheap.

description: A very small pistol chambered in .32 ACP.  Due to its intended role, it lacks any sights.

variants.description: A very small pistol chambered in .32 ACP.  It lacks any sort of sights as it's designed for very close engagement distances.

description: A semi-automatic smooth-bore shotgun, this weapon features respectable ergonomics and room for 5 shotshells within its tubular magazine.  Suitable both for competition shooting and offensive combat roles, this firearm is faster to shoot than a traditional pump-action model, with the caveat that it's harder to maintain than its manually operated siblings as it relies on an internal gas system to cycle rounds rather than the user's own physical input.

variants.description: Benelli's first gas-operated shotgun, featuring dual pistons for enhanced reliability with various loads and a collapsible buttstock that reduces length by almost 8 inches.  Adopted in 1999 as the M1014 Joint Service Combat Shotgun, the Benelli M4 is one of the finest combat shotguns available.

description: A compact light-weight steel and polymer magazine for use with Glock pistols chambered for 10mm Auto.
A compact light-weight steel and polymer magazine for use with Glock pistols chambered for 10mm Auto, limited to 10 rounds.
A stubby steel and polymer magazine for use with the compact Glock pistol.  It is too short to insert into most other 10mm Glock pattern magwells.

variants.description: A stubby steel and polymer magazine for use with the Glock 29.  It is too short to insert into most other 10mm Glock pattern magwells.

description: A 30-round plastic and steel box 10mm Auto magazine for use with Glock pistols and submachine guns chambered in 10mm.

variants.description: A 30-round plastic and steel box 10mm Auto magazine for use with Glock pattern magazine wells, namely the Vector SDP.

//:: I couldn't find much conclusive data, but by all accounts it seems to share the dimensions of the G19's magazine.
description: A compact, light-weight polymer magazine for use with the Glock 44.

name.str: 微型隐蔽携带手枪弹匣

description: A 6 round magazine for the micro conceal carry pistol.

variants.description: A 6 round magazine for the LWS 32.

description: A standard 6-round capacity magazine for the Glock 42.

//: Audited with ImageJ.  Weight from 15rd mag ratio.
description: An extended 22-round magazine for use with Glock pistols chambered for .40 S&W.

//: Audited with ImageJ.  Weight from manufacturer.
description: A compact, light-weight polymer magazine for use with Glock pistols chambered for .40 S&W.
A compact, light-weight polymer magazine for use with Glock pistols chambered for .40 S&W.  It has been limited to 10 rounds to comply with firearm laws.
A compact, light-weight polymer magazine with a 9 round capacity for use with Glock pistols chambered for .40 S&W.

//: Dimensions via ImageJ.  Weight from vendor.
description: An aftermarket 30-round polymer and steel box magazine designed for use with the Glock pistol.

variants.description: A 30-round polymer and steel box magazine for use with the KRISS Vector.

//: Audited with ImageJ.  Weight from Manufacturer.
description: A 13-round magazine for use with Glock firearms chambered in .45 ACP.

variants.description: A standard capacity magazine for use with the Glock 21.

//: Audited with ImageJ.  Weight from Manufacturer.
description: A 10-round magazine for use with Glock firearms chambered in .45 ACP.

//: Audited with ImageJ.  Weight from Vendor.
description: A 26-round magazine for use with Glock firearms chambered in .45 ACP.

variants.description: A double-capacity magazine for use with the Glock 21.

description: A 6-round magazine for the Glock 36. Incompatible with other .45 Glocks
An extended 33-round polymer magazine for use with Glock compatible firearms.
A compact, light-weight polymer magazine for use with Glock compatible firearms.
A small 6-round magazine for the Glock 43.  Incompatible with the Glock 43X.
A 10-round magazine for the Glock 43X and the Glock 48.  Incompatible with the Glock 43.
A compact, light-weight polymer magazine for use with the Glock 19, which has been limited to 10 rounds to comply with firearm regulations.
Made for the Glock 17, this magazine holds 17 rounds.
Made for the Glock 17, this magazine holds 10 rounds to comply with firearm regulations.
Made for the Glock 17, this cheap-looking aftermarket magazine holds 22 rounds.
Made to fit most Glock pistols.  This drum magazine holds 50 rounds.
Made to fit most Glock pistols.  This double drum magazine holds 100 rounds.
